title: GetResponse vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse vs Buttondown. See pricing, features, ratings, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: GetResponse vs Buttondown: Complete Email Marketing Comparison
intro: GetResponse and Buttondown are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. GetResponse is a comprehensive all-in-one marketing suite with webinars and courses, while Buttondown focuses on simplicity and markdown-first writing for developers and writers. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your newsletter or marketing strategy.

comparison_table.tool_a: Complete marketing platform with webinars, courses, and funnels
comparison_table.tool_b: Markdown writing and API access for developers

comparison_table.tool_a: Online course creators and webinar hosts

choose_a_if: You need webinar hosting, landing pages, and online course functionality built-in
You're building sales funnels or conversion-focused marketing campaigns
You want a larger free tier with 500 subscribers included
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown format
You need API access and technical integration capabilities
You value simpler pricing and a more streamlined, focused tool

faq.a: It depends on your needs. GetResponse is better for marketers needing a complete platform with webinars and courses, while Buttondown is better for developers and writers who prefer simplicity and markdown. Buttondown also has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.8 vs 4.2 on G2).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown's paid plans start at $9/month compared to GetResponse's $19/month, making it less expensive. However, GetResponse's free tier allows 500 subscribers versus Buttondown's 100, which may provide better value for growing newsletters.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms offer import/export functionality. Buttondown specifically includes import/export as a key feature, allowing you to migrate your subscriber lists and archives. You may need to manually recreate your email templates in GetResponse's different editor.
